title: Derive a Student from Person
prompt_md:
## Goal

The base class `Person` below is given (do not modify it). Write a class `Student` that publicly inherits from it. `Person` keeps its data private and exposes it only through getters, so a `Student` can be created only by handing the name and age to `Person`'s constructor -- you must delegate to it from `Student`'s constructor.

## Input

- Line 1: a string `name` (no spaces) and an integer `age`.
- Line 2: an integer `studentId`.

## Output

Exactly three lines, every value obtained by calling getters on a single `Student` object:

```
Name: <name>
Age: <age>
Student ID: <studentId>
```

## Constraints

- `Person` stores `name` and `age` as **private** members and provides only getters for them. It has no default constructor.
- `Student` adds its own private `studentId` and must initialize the inherited name and age by delegating to `Person`'s constructor (it cannot touch those private members directly).

title: Vehicle hierarchy with inherited behavior
prompt_md:
## Goal

The `Vehicle` base class below is given (do not modify it); it holds shared data privately and exposes it through getters. Write two classes `Car` and `Truck` that publicly inherit from it and each add one specialized member. Each derived constructor must delegate the shared make/year to `Vehicle`'s constructor.

## Input

- Line 1: a string `carMake` (no spaces), an integer `carYear`, and an integer `numDoors`.
- Line 2: a string `truckMake` (no spaces), an integer `truckYear`, and a double `payloadTons`.

## Output

Exactly two lines:

```
Car: <carMake> (<carYear>), <numDoors> doors
Truck: <truckMake> (<truckYear>), <payloadTons>t payload
```

`payloadTons` is printed with exactly one decimal place.

## Constraints

- `Vehicle` stores make and year privately with getters and has no default constructor.
- `Car` adds a door count; `Truck` adds a payload in tons. Both reach make/year only through the inherited getters, and both initialize the inherited portion by delegating to `Vehicle`'s constructor.

test_cases.label: Common vehicles
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Car: Toyota (2022), 4 doors
Truck: Ford (2020), 5.5t payload

test_cases.label: Sports car and heavy truck
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Car: Porsche (2024), 2 doors
Truck: Volvo (2019), 18.0t payload

test_cases.label: Edge values
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Car: Mini (1960), 2 doors
Truck: Mack (2100), 100.0t payload
